title: Investigate suspicious JNDI traffic patterns in payment-svc logs
description:
Starting ~2021-12-08, security scanner flagged outbound LDAP connections from payment-svc-prod pods. Connections attempted to external IPs not in the allowlist. The log format shows '${jndi:ldap://...}' strings appearing in User-Agent headers being logged by the application.

This may be related to the log4j JNDI injection vulnerability disclosed on 2021-12-09 as CVE-2021-44228.

title: Upgrade log4j-core to 2.15.0 to remediate CVE-2021-44228
description:
Full remediation for Log4Shell vulnerability.

Steps:
1. Upgrade log4j-core from 2.14.1 → 2.15.0 in parent pom.xml
2. Verify all services pick up the new version
3. Run full regression test suite
4. Deploy to staging, verify no JNDI lookups in logs
5. Deploy to production with staged rollout

Interim mitigation: -Dlog4j2.formatMsgNoLookups=true is live (see PR-2940).

title: Add WAF rules to block JNDI lookup strings in HTTP headers
Audit all services for log4j version — CVE-2021-44228 blast radius
description:
Identify all microservices in the platform using log4j-core < 2.15.0.

Initial scan results:
- payment-svc: log4j-core 2.14.1 (VULNERABLE)
- notification-svc: log4j-core 2.13.3 (VULNERABLE)
- reporting-svc: log4j-core 2.12.1 (VULNERABLE — different branch)
- auth-svc: logback (NOT affected)
- order-svc: log4j-core 2.15.0 (SAFE — already upgraded)

Priority: payment-svc first (P0 incident active), then notification and reporting.

title: Dependency audit: flag log4j-core 2.14.1 upgrade for security review
description:
Routine dependency audit note.
log4j-core was upgraded from 2.13.3 to 2.14.1 in PR-2847 (merged 2021-11-28).
Upgrade was for performance — no security review was conducted as no CVE existed at merge time.

Closing as no-action — upgrade was valid at time of merge.
